feedback.text: I would like for this legislation to also cover the destruction of video games. I know the European Union likes to score easy wins on simple to come up with, pro-consumer regulation, and this is the perfect time and place to do it on a problem where the answer is long overdue Massive companies revoking people's access to products they have already paid for is outrageous. Can you imagine opening a book after a few years and see the pages blank? I can't. I also can't imagine why hostile developers are allowed to get away with this. They place a tiny little clause in their terms and conditions (which, let's be honest, nobody reads anyway) saying they're allowed to do exactly that, even though current EU laws make that clause null and void. But they do it anyway, and they do it irresponsibly, because they get away with it. It's time to make that stop once and for all One does not have to look far to see how we, the little people, view this state of things. The Citizens Initiative on ending the destruction of videogames has amassed an astounding 1.5 MILLION signatures as of the writing of this feedback, and I was among some of the first to sign a year ago. The entire Stop Killing Games movement itself has explored several ways on how to tackle this issue fairly, so even doing the bare minimum homework could make this legislation very effective. This would positively affect not only us lucky few living in Europe, but also people around the entire globe - that's how much weight this could hold These anti-consumer companies will, of course, try to fight back because they're afraid that their greedy selves will lose a way to milk money out of us. They have already begun lobbying against the intiative, making the irrelevant 'seatbelt argument' and such, and even taking potshots behind the scenes. The entire gaming industry itself is on a very bad trajectory in general, and has been for way too many years. Help us fight back, and make these massive companies pay for the damage they do! This is just a drop in the bucket in the gaming industry when it comes to its problems (and I urge you to take this fight seriously on all fronts later on, not just this one), but even this would be a huge win not only for consumers, but for the EU's reputation on the world stage. Please cover this issue in the Digital Fairness Act, and end the destruction of our beloved memories!
